title: Scoring and Feedback Rules
general_rules: Show percentile-style language only if normed data exists. Otherwise use low / medium / high preference or strength bands.
Return 3 strengths and 2 growth suggestions, not a harsh judgment.
Always include the banner that this is an educational self-assessment, not a clinical instrument.

openness.high: You may enjoy novelty, creativity, and exploratory learning.
openness.mid: You may balance curiosity with practicality.
openness.low: You may prefer familiar methods and clear expectations.

conscientiousness.high: You may thrive with structured plans, checklists, and clear goals.
conscientiousness.mid: You may adapt between structure and flexibility.
conscientiousness.low: You may benefit from more visible routines and reminders.

extraversion.high: You may learn well through discussion, speaking, and group momentum.
extraversion.mid: You may shift comfortably between solo and group work.
extraversion.low: You may prefer quiet reflection before sharing ideas.

agreeableness.high: You may value harmony, teamwork, and supportive communication.
agreeableness.mid: You may balance cooperation with directness.
agreeableness.low: You may be more blunt, skeptical, or debate-oriented.

emotional_stability.high: You may stay steadier under pressure and recover faster from setbacks.
emotional_stability.mid: You may handle some stress well while still needing recovery time.
emotional_stability.low: You may benefit from stress-management habits and pacing tools.

eq_feedback_domains.self_awareness: Noticing your emotional state helps you choose better responses.
eq_feedback_domains.self_regulation: Pausing before reacting can improve judgment and performance.
eq_feedback_domains.empathy: Reading other people well supports stronger relationships and teamwork.
eq_feedback_domains.social_awareness: Understanding group dynamics helps in school, work, and leadership.
eq_feedback_domains.relationship_management: Handling conflict well is a major practical life skill.
